behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: taxonomy source/config + request builder + API client payload wiring. A unit test of only a local formatter can miss dropped labels during serialization or adapter mapping. Rejected System because behavior text is about request content, not real network/OS effects; rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a stub API transport that records outbound payloads. Feed a taxonomy with multiple labels and assert every configured label appears in the actual dispatched request body (including ordering/field path expected by the provider contract).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is local decision logic (membership check + fallback mapping) and can fail entirely within one function/object boundary with no I/O. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on transformation rule for a returned label, not inter-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/validation function with taxonomy {legal, technical, financial, other} and raw label "medical" from a fixture response object; assert output is exactly "unknown" and never the raw value.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Determinism of normalization is pure local logic; no external boundary is required to expose the failure. Rejected higher levels because behavior explicitly scopes to identical raw responses and the module’s normalization step.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (e.g., 100 iterations) with the exact same raw response fixture containing casing/whitespace variants and assert identical final label every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency of model-produced labels, which depends on real model behavior/non-determinism. A stub would hide this class of failure. Rejected Workflow because this is a single agentic decision repeated, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls (same document, same taxonomy, fixed prompt/template/version settings), capture provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label stability whenever raw provider output is equivalent; flag drift cases where model output changes without input/config changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure typically appears at boundary between config, tokenizer/counting component, and request dispatcher (pre-dispatch gate). Unit tests on only truncation logic can miss miswired enforcement before send. Rejected System because real network is not needed to prove pre-dispatch enforcement; rejected Agentic because model output quality is irrelevant.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate real request-construction path with tokenizer/count estimator and a spy transport. Provide over-budget input and assert policy action (truncate or reject) occurs before transport send; verify no over-budget payload is dispatched.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real provider-reported usage metadata from an external API; in-process fakes can trivially pass and miss real tokenization/accounting differences. Rejected Agentic because assertion is about usage accounting, not classification quality/decision correctness from model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API calls with representative documents and parse provider usage fields from actual HTTP responses; assert reported prompt/total tokens are <= configured budget for each case.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is retry orchestration across API adapter + backoff/timer + caller contract; reproducible with controlled stub responses and clock. Rejected System because real network/rate-limit infra is not required to expose retry-state bugs; rejected Agentic because model output content is irrelevant.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API that returns rate-limit error (e.g., 429/Retry-After) on first call and success on second; run module with deterministic clock/backoff and assert it waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing the first error.
